citation_1_title: Fishery and Aquaculture Country Profile: Lesotho
citation_1_locator: pp. 1-2 and 5: capture fisheries and rural aquaculture are subsistence-type; national capture fisheries totaled 30-45 tonnes in 1997-2006; employment is very limited and mostly part-time.
citation_2_title: Strategies for fish production in lowlands reservoirs, Lesotho
citation_2_locator: Abstract and contents: Lesotho's lowland reservoir fisheries were underexploited, and the Fisheries Section formulated strategies for local community groups, entrepreneurs, and part-time commercial gillnet fisheries.
notes: Applied a one-wharf floor because Lesotho maintains a real but very small inland fishing lane in reservoirs and rivers; the fishery stays part-time and low-output, so the public row rises only to 1 rather than a coastal-scale cap. | The documented-working floor keeps Fishing at 1 after the formula resolves to zero; the audited state remains landlocked and low-output, but the in-state freshwater fishery is real. | Lesotho remains a tiny inland fishing lane rather than a coast-scale fishery, but its lowland reservoirs and rivers do sustain real capture-fisheries working inside the Drakensberg footprint.

citation_1_locator: Sweetwaters managed only a two-tonne pig-iron trial in 1901 before limestone scarcity halted it, Alverstone liquidated in 1911, and the stronger Dundee-Utrecht/Newcastle chain becomes commercial only from 1926 in KwaZulu-Natal.
citation_2_title: Water and Wastewater Management in the Iron and Steel Industry (Ed. 1)
citation_2_locator: p. 21: Pretoria West's ferrous ore mine produced about 4,000 tonnes of pig iron between 1918 and 1921, which is the current West Transvaal bounded-row benchmark.
notes: Retained a zero iron row because the rechecked Natal experiments and later Dundee-Utrecht/Newcastle chain remain off-footprint and still fall short of the current West Transvaal Pretoria threshold for even a bounded Drakensberg iron row. | Pass 9 recheck kept the hard zero after comparing the off-footprint Natal ironwork sequence with the package's one-cap Pretoria benchmark: Sweetwaters was only a brief 1901 trial, Alverstone liquidated in 1911, and the stronger Dundee-Utrecht/Newcastle chain begins from the KwaZulu-Natal side in 1926. | Sweetwaters, Alverstone, and the Dundee-Utrecht/Newcastle chain remain KwaZulu-Natal localities outside Drakensberg; even against the accepted one-cap Pretoria standard, they still do not yield a Lesotho-localized commercial iron series.
